step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the previous agent's assessment. Although the term "above duramater" may not accurately describe the exact relationship of Batson’s veins to the dura mater, it is indeed the closest description among the available options. These veins typically run just under the periosteum of the vertebrae before entering the subarachnoid space, making “in vertebral canal above duramater” a reasonable approximation from a clinical standpoint.
